channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Edo Police Arrest Suspected Kidnappers, Recover Weapons During Operations
item.description: Na serious operation wey Edo State Police Command run don lead to the arrest of three people wey dem suspect say dem dey do kidnapping for di state. One of di suspects na person wey come from Niger Re...

item.title: B.C. initiative raises funds for portable technology to detect invasive mussels
item.description: Invasive quagga and zebra mussels pose a significant threat to British Columbia's waterways, potentially harming water quality, beaches, and local tourism. To prevent these mussels from spreading into...

item.title: Tragic incident in Madhya Pradesh: Mother kills four daughters and takes her own life
item.description: A tragic event unfolded in Khamaria village, Madhya Pradesh, leaving the local community in shock. A 30-year-old woman, Savita Lodhi, reportedly threw her four young daughters, aged from a few months ...

item.title: Harman warns Starmer over potential inconsistencies in Mandelson appointment records
item.description: Harriet Harman has warned Sir Keir Starmer that he could face accusations of misleading the House of Commons regarding his appointment of Lord Peter Mandelson as US ambassador if his statements do not...

item.title: Canada commits $35B to strengthen Arctic defence and develop Northern infrastructure
item.description: Prime Minister Mark Carney has unveiled a $35 billion federal plan aimed at strengthening Canada's Arctic sovereignty and improving infrastructure across the Northern territories. The initiative seeks...

item.title: Most Filipinos Support Anti-Political Dynasty Law, Pulse Asia Survey Shows
item.description: According to a recent Pulse Asia survey released on March 13, 2026, a substantial 64% of Filipinos support the passage of an anti-political dynasty law, a 10-percentage point increase from the 54% rec...

item.title: Court of Appeal Validates Wole Oluyede as PDP Governorship Candidate in Ekiti
item.description: The Court of Appeal for Akure, Ondo State, don uphold the primary election wey PDP hold for Ekiti State, wey select Wole Oluyede as their governorship candidate for the 20 June election. The three-jud...

item.title: Study Warns Pakistan Vulnerable to Oil Price Shocks Amid Middle East Tensions
item.description: A recent study by Prof Abida Naurin of the Pakistan Institute of Development Economics has raised concerns over Pakistan's vulnerability to rising Middle East tensions. The research points out that es...

item.title: R.N. Ravi takes oath as 22nd Governor of West Bengal amid political discussions
item.description: On 12th March 2026, Rabindra Narayan Ravi was formally sworn in as the 22nd Governor of West Bengal at a ceremony held in Lok Bhavan, Kolkata. The oath was administered by Chief Justice Sujoy Pal of t...

item.title: Russia Gains Economically Amid Iran Conflict Due to Rising Oil Prices
item.description: The ongoing conflict in Iran has had unintended economic consequences for Russia, positioning it as a financial beneficiary despite not being a direct participant in the hostilities. US-Israeli strike...

item.title: Government outlines key priorities and projects under Vision 2050
item.description: As Tanzania prepares to launch its Development Vision 2050 in July, the government has outlined eight priority areas under the Fourth Five-Year National Development Plan to boost inclusive economic gr...

item.title: Maryborough childcare centre fined after toddler dies during nap
item.description: A childcare centre in Maryborough, Queensland, has been fined $15,000 following an investigation into the death of a two-year-old boy during nap time in July 2022. The boy was found unresponsive at Li...

item.title: Rising Competition and Risk in Global Hydrocarbon Shipments
item.description: Recent reports indicate that several liquefied natural gas (LNG) carriers transporting Nigerian and U.S. gas have recently altered their original destinations, redirecting shipments toward Asian ports...

item.title: Trump Administration Juggles Iran War Decisions Amid Rising Oil Prices
item.description: Inside the White House, there is a real tug-of-war over the Iran conflict. US President Donald Trump’s statements on the war keep changing, reflecting disagreements among his aides on how and when to ...

item.title: Regina Resident Escapes December House Fire Thanks to Smoke Detector
item.description: Jimmy Ottenbreit, a resident of Regina, experienced a life-changing event last December when his home caught fire. Having lived alone for the past three years after losing his wife and daughter to can...

item.title: Victor Moses Stuck in Dubai Amid Iran Conflict, Nigeria Starts Citizen Evacuation
item.description: Victor Moses, former Super Eagles winger, don dey stranded for Dubai because of the ongoing tensions between Iran, United States, and Israel wey don disrupt air travel across some parts of the Middle ...

item.title: UK government vows to prevent unfair price hikes amid rising oil costs
item.description: The UK government has declared it will not tolerate energy companies taking advantage of surging oil prices amid the ongoing Middle East conflict. Energy Secretary Ed Miliband stated that the Competit...

item.title: Kenya Struggles with Worsening Food Insecurity and Child Malnutrition in Arid Regions
item.description: Kenya is currently undergoing a serious food insecurity and malnutrition crisis, especially in the 23 Arid and Semi-Arid Lands (ASAL) counties. Latest reports estimate that 3.3 million Kenyans are in ...

item.title: Makhanda man charged with attempted murder after alleged acid attack on ex-girlfriend
item.description: A 53-year-old man from Makhanda, Eastern Cape, has been arrested and charged with attempted murder after allegedly beating and dousing his ex-girlfriend with acid in a brutal attack on 20 February. Th...

item.title: Government Urged to Act on Potential Fuel Supply Disruptions
item.description: The editorial warns that Kenya faces a serious threat of fuel shortages due to rising tensions in the Middle East, where most of our petroleum imports come from. It calls on the government to take swi...
